spec_match.rationale: Renders a synthetic HDR gradient into two canvases with different tonemap operators — the closest thing the platform has to a tonemapping demo on top of the float16 colour type.

interactive.rationale: Two range inputs and two selects drive live tonemap operator selection and gradient parameters.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises a synthetic HDR gradient and side-by-side canvases; page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Tonemap operators are baked-in functions; doesn't expose Reinhard vs ACES vs PQ tradeoffs as a sliding parameter. Also no out-of-gamut detection.

design_system.rationale: Page styling via variables; raw `#fdfcf8` and `#000` are inside canvas drawing code (content, not design tokens) which is acceptable.

openQuestions.title: Surface tonemap parameters
openQuestions.detail: ACES and Reinhard both take a `white-point` parameter that authors will want to tune. Expose it as a slider so the demo teaches not just the API but the tonemapping concept.

openQuestions.title: Add a `which pixels clipped` overlay
openQuestions.detail: Toggle that paints a red overlay where the unorm8 lane clipped to 1.0 — concrete visualisation of the float16 advantage.

summary: Most ambitious concept in the feature — synthetic HDR gradient with two tonemap operators side by side. Add a clipping-overlay toggle to drive the point home.
